ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic paneer dish class; no specific Kadai Paneer entry exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a dedicated Kadai Paneer code. A0CKP (cheese-based dish) is the closest defensible parent category.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code exists for Kadai Paneer or a sufficiently close parent food concept.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical restaurant serving of Kadai Paneer is approximately 300 g, comprising paneer cubes in a thick spiced tomato-onion-capsicum gravy, usually served alongside naan or rice.

nutrient_source.title: Paneer curry (Indian cottage cheese curry) — ICMR-NIN Nutritive Value of Indian Foods
nutrient_source.organization: Indian Council of Medical Research – National Institute of Nutrition (ICMR-NIN)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: ICMR-NIN reports values for paneer curry (cottage cheese cooked with tomato, onion, and spices). Kadai Paneer is a specific variant of paneer curry cooked in a wok (kadai) with capsicum and whole spices. Values are adapted from the ICMR-NIN paneer curry entry and cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central entries for paneer and component ingredients to reflect the spicy restaurant-style preparation. Energy and macros are consistent with published ranges for restaurant-style Kadai Paneer (approximately 150–180 kcal/100 g).

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values 2021 — paneer / cottage cheese and mixed vegetable curry analogues
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Kadai Paneer in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI is estimated at 35 based on the closest analogous foods: paneer (cottage cheese, GI ~30) and mixed vegetable curry with tomato-onion base (GI ~35–40). The dominant macronutrient is fat and protein from paneer, with moderate carbohydrate from vegetables and gravy. A GI of 35 is a conservative mid-point estimate consistent with published values for similar paneer-based curry dishes. GL per serving = (35 × 8.2 × 300) / (100 × 100) = 8.61, rounded to 9.7 using the adapted per-serving carbohydrate of 27.7 g (8.2 g/100 g × 300 g / 100 × 35 / 100 × 300 = 8.61 — corrected: GL = GI/100 × carb_per_serving_g = 0.35 × 24.6 = 8.61 ≈ 8.6). GL recalculated: carb per serving = 8.2 g/100 g × 300 g = 24.6 g; GL = (35 × 24.6) / 100 = 8.61, reported as 8.6.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~320 mg/100 g) from added salt and spice blends in restaurant preparation
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to 150–200 g and request reduced salt preparation, as a standard 300 g restaurant serving contributes approximately 960 mg sodium, representing 42% of the 2300 mg daily limit.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index (~35) driven by high protein and fat content from paneer, with moderate carbohydrate from vegetables and gravy
type2_diabetes.guidance: Kadai Paneer has a low GI of approximately 35 and a glycemic load of ~8.6 per 300 g serving, making it a suitable choice for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when consumed with a low-GI staple such as roti or cauliflower rice rather than white rice or naan.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat content (~5.8 g/100 g) from paneer and cooking oil, contributing approximately 17.4 g saturated fat per 300 g serving
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should moderate portion size due to the saturated fat contribution from paneer and oil; choosing a preparation with less oil and smaller paneer portions can reduce saturated fat intake toward the recommended limit of less than 20 g per day.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Kadai Paneer is a popular North Indian vegetarian dish consisting of cubes of paneer (fresh Indian cottage cheese) cooked with onions, capsicum (bell pepper), tomatoes, ginger, garlic, and a blend of whole and ground spices including coriander seeds, cumin, dried red chillies, and garam masala. The dish is traditionally prepared and served in a kadai, a thick circular iron wok used in Indian cooking, which gives the dish its name. The cooking process involves dry-roasting whole spices, sautéing aromatics, building a tomato-onion gravy, and finishing with paneer cubes and capsicum to retain texture. The result is a semi-dry to moderately saucy dish with a bold, smoky, and spicy flavour profile, a slightly charred aroma from the wok, and a creamy-firm texture from the paneer. Kadai Paneer is a staple of North Indian restaurant menus and is widely served across India, Pakistan, and the Indian diaspora globally, including Singapore. Nutritionally, it is a moderate-energy dish (~165 kcal/100 g) with a good protein contribution from paneer (~7.8 g/100 g), significant fat (~11.5 g/100 g, of which ~5.8 g is saturated), and moderate carbohydrate (~8.2 g/100 g). It is a meaningful source of calcium (~185 mg/100 g) and vitamin B12 (~0.3 mcg/100 g) from the dairy component. Its low glycemic index (~35) makes it suitable for blood glucose management when paired with appropriate staples. The dish is naturally gluten-free and vegetarian, and is halal when prepared without alcohol-based ingredients.
common_user_queries: How many calories in Kadai Paneer?
Is Kadai Paneer good for diabetics?
What spices are in Kadai Paneer?
Kadai Paneer vs Paneer Butter Masala difference
Is Kadai Paneer high in sodium?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from ICMR-NIN paneer curry data and cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central component data. No exact Kadai Paneer entry exists in a primary approved source. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database using paneer and mixed vegetable curry analogues. Confidence is medium rather than high because no single approved source provides a direct Kadai Paneer nutritional profile.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted from ICMR-NIN Nutritive Value of Indian Foods (paneer curry entry) and cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central (paneer, tomato, onion, capsicum components). No exact Kadai Paneer entry exists in any primary approved source; this is an adapted estimate. 2. GI of 35 is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database values for paneer (~30) and mixed vegetable curry (~35–40); no direct GI measurement for Kadai Paneer is available in the approved GI database. 3. Glycemic load per serving recalculated as: GL = (GI/100) × carbohydrate_per_serving_g = (35/100) × 24.6 g = 8.61, reported as 8.6. 4. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the ICMR-NIN source for this food category. 5. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the ICMR-NIN source for this food category. 6. The food_name in the input JSON contained a typographical error ('Spciy' instead of 'Spicy'); the canonical food_name has been corrected to 'Kadai Paneer' (the spicy variant descriptor is captured in aliases). 7. FoodOn URI FOODON_03311069 is used as closest parent (paneer dish class); no exact Kadai Paneer FoodOn entry exists. FoodEx2 code A0CKP is used as closest parent for cheese-based dishes. 8. Serving size of 300 g is based on typical North Indian restaurant portion norms consistent with ICMR-NIN serving guidance for curry dishes.
